title: Return structured parse result from ParseItemSpec (distinguish NotFound vs Malformed)
description:
## Evidence
`MemorySmithItemRegistry.ParseItemSpec()` returns `null` for all failure modes: missing fields, empty content, unparseable lines. No structured error distinguishes NotFound from Malformed.

## Impact
A typo in a registry page silently becomes "item not found." The lookup path gives no clue whether the source page exists but is malformed.

## Fix
1. Return structured result type (`ItemSpecParseResult`) with states: Success, NotFound, Malformed(missingFields)
2. Log the page ID and missing fields when required metadata is absent
3. Add tests for incomplete item pages and malformed front matter

## Verification
Verified against codebase 2026-06-23. See `Data/Pages/audits/msa-audit-verification-6-23.md` section F7.
